title: senecio mapuche
intro: Senecio mapuche is a plant species native to Chile and Argentina, also known as Andean wormwood or Mapuche wormwood, which belongs to the daisy family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily containing sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Senecio mapuche has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The herbal preparations of Senecio mapuche include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
